title: sporobolus indicus subsp. pallidior
intro: Sporobolus indicus subsp. pallidior, also known as the Indian grass, is a species of grass native to India and Southeast Asia, characterized by its pale yellow or white inflorescences and slender leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic compounds. The health benefits of Sporobolus indicus subsp. pallidior include its potential to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, as well as its ability to protect against certain diseases, such as diabetes and cardiovascular disease. The plant can be prepared into various herbal products, including teas, infusions, and capsules, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
